prediction.reasoning: As an AI, I cannot predict future stock prices with certainty, and this is not financial advice. This prediction is generated using a simplified Geometric Brownian Motion model, which assumes that stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Due to the absence of specific historical data for TFC, I have used generalized, illustrative equity market parameters: an assumed average annual return (drift) of 8% and an assumed annual volatility of 20%. These parameters were scaled for the 21-day period. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal distribution. The confidence intervals reflect the range within which the price is expected to fall based on these assumptions. This is a theoretical exercise using generalized market behavior and does not incorporate any specific fundamental analysis, real-time market news, or company-specific events, all of which significantly influence actual stock prices. Stock market predictions are inherently uncertain, and actual prices may deviate substantially from these estimates.
